message:
feat(abi): port 10 nis_* name helpers + clone stubs (bd-79a07)

Continue NIS+ libnsl parity. Ten more entries built around real
NIS+-name string handling plus three null-returning clone stubs.

Real name-handling helpers (7) — each has both a TLS-backed
*_of(name) form and a buffered _r(name, *buf, buflen) form:

  - nis_domain_of / nis_domain_of_r — strip the first label of a
    NIS+ name ("host.subdom.dom." -> "subdom.dom."). Handles
    backslash-escaped dots correctly via a small first-unescaped-dot
    helper.
  - nis_leaf_of / nis_leaf_of_r — return the first label only
    ("host.subdom.dom." -> "host"). Treats a name with no dots as
    its own leaf.
  - nis_name_of / nis_name_of_r — the part of a name relative to
    the local NIS+ directory. Since our nis_local_directory()
    returns the empty string, behaves as a verbatim pass-through
    (callers see the input as-is).

Comparator (1):
  - nis_dir_cmp(a, b) -> nis_compare_t — case-insensitive ASCII
    compare with one trailing-dot tolerance per NIS+ convention.
    Returns 0 (LOWER_NAME) / 1 (SAME_NAME) / 2 (HIGHER_NAME) /
    3 (NOT_SEQUENTIAL on NULL inputs).

Clone stubs (3):
  - nis_clone_directory / nis_clone_object / nis_clone_result —
    stubs returning NULL since our query layer never produces real
    NIS+ result objects to clone.

Both _r variants enforce ERANGE on overflow + EINVAL on NULL buf;
the *_of TLS variants store into per-thread RefCell<Vec<u8>> so
the returned pointer is stable until the next call on the same
thread.

Tests: 9 abi tests covering single-label edge cases, NULL inputs,
caller buffer round-trips, ERANGE on too-small buffer, case-
insensitive equality with mixed case + trailing-dot tolerance,
LOWER/HIGHER/NOT_SEQUENTIAL classification, and NULL clone returns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-747","title":"Stub wave 1: getenv/setenv/unsetenv + environment invariants","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- Implement getenv/setenv/unsetenv semantics with thread-safety policy and strict/hardened parity notes.
- Add fixture tests for overwrite flags, invalid names, and concurrent reads.

Acceptance:
- Environment fixtures match glibc behavior for covered cases.
- Symbols removed from top-stub list and support matrix upgraded accordingly.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.830918180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:09.887086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","env","stubs"]}
 {"id":"bd-761b","title":"Conformance: direct execute spawn_exec_ops fixture cases","description":"Add direct execute_fixture_case coverage for spawn_exec_ops fixtures so posix_spawn, execve, and system cases are checked against the shared executor API directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T13:14:22.788257943Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T13:22:56.014823852Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T13:22:56.012335843Z","close_reason":"Added direct execute_fixture_case coverage for spawn_exec_ops fixtures; verified focused test/check/clippy via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","process","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-78nb4","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] strtod missed errno=ERANGE on overflow/underflow","description":"FOUND + FIXED via differential harness vs glibc (commit 0bd439d2).

strtod("1e400", &end) returned +Infinity (correct) but failed to set errno=ERANGE (POSIX violation). Same for strtod("1e-400", &end) which returned 0.0 without ERANGE. Glibc sets ERANGE in both cases.

Reachability: any program that uses strtod to parse numeric input and relies on errno to distinguish overflow-Inf from literal "INF" (or underflow-0 from literal "0") would silently misclassify. Common in JSON/config parsers and scientific code.

Fix in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/stdlib_abi.rs strtod(): after parsing, set errno=ERANGE if val.is_infinite() AND consumed prefix doesn't contain "inf" literal, OR val==0.0 AND consumed prefix contains any non-zero digit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T04:15:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 0bd439d2; differential test diff_strtod_cases now passes including 1e400/1e-400 ERANGE cases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","errno","high","review","stdlib"]}
+{"id":"bd-79a07","title":"PORT: 10 nis_* name helpers + clone stubs (nis_domain_of/leaf_of/name_of + _r variants + nis_dir_cmp + 3 clone stubs)","description":"Continue NIS+ libnsl parity. Ship 10 nis_* name-handling helpers + clone stubs. (1) nis_domain_of(name) -> *mut c_char: pointer into a thread-local static returning the name with its first label stripped (e.g. "host.subdom.dom." -> "subdom.dom."). (2) nis_domain_of_r(name, *buf, buflen) -> *mut c_char: same into caller buffer; ERANGE on overflow. (3) nis_leaf_of(name) -> *mut c_char: pointer into a thread-local returning the first label only ("host.subdom.dom." -> "host"). (4) nis_leaf_of_r(name, *buf, buflen): same into caller buffer. (5) nis_name_of(name): returns the "name part" with the local domain stripped; since our nis_local_directory is empty, behave as pass-through. (6) nis_name_of_r(name, *buf, buflen): pass-through into caller buffer. (7) nis_dir_cmp(a, b) -> int: case-insensitive compare of two NIS+ directory names; return 0/1/-1/-2 (LOWER_NAME / SAME_NAME / HIGHER_NAME / NOT_SEQUENTIAL) per nis_compare_t. (8)/(9)/(10) nis_clone_directory / nis_clone_object / nis_clone_result: stubs returning NULL.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:31:36.968454969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:31:36.968454969Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-79ctp","title":"PORT: __nss_lookup_function + __nss_hosts_lookup2 + __nss_next2 (NSS-internal stubs)","description":"Port the remaining NSS-internal stub symbols missing from our libc surface: __nss_lookup_function (resolve a per-service function pointer in the NSS module loader; we have no NSS modules so always returns NULL), __nss_hosts_lookup2 (newer "v2" hosts lookup variant with explicit address-family + extended errno; UNAVAIL stub), __nss_next2 (advance to next NSS service with fallback-name selector; UNAVAIL stub). Same fail-graceful pattern as the already-shipped __nss_passwd_lookup / __nss_group_lookup / __nss_hosts_lookup / __nss_next family in glibc_internal_abi. Tests verify each stub resolves and returns the expected NSS_STATUS_UNAVAIL (-1) or NULL pointer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:31:32.748916381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:34:51.507055259Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:34:51.504862777Z","close_reason":"shipped: __nss_lookup_function + __nss_hosts_lookup2 + __nss_next2 NSS-internal UNAVAIL stubs matching existing pattern + 3 abi tests, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-79jdn","title":"syscall: renameat2 exchange with null oldpath should return EFAULT exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EFAULT for renameat2(AT_FDCWD, NULL, AT_FDCWD, ".", RENAME_EXCHANGE). Add ABI regression coverage so exchange mode preserves the null-source-path fault.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:29:11.574218298Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T04:18:08.162609084Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T04:18:08.160145681Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-79sxl","title":"FUZZ: statx extended-mask + flags structure-aware harness","description":"crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_statx.rs. Fuzzes dirfd (AT_FDCWD/-1/INT_MAX), path (seed list + fuzz bytes), mask (u32), flags (AT_*). 32-byte guard bands on both sides of a 256-byte statx buffer, verified intact post-call. Dev-note: initial harness had a too-strict 'stx_mask subset of requested' invariant; Linux always fills STATX_BASIC_STATS regardless of request_mask, so the invariant was removed after first-run crash discovery. 5.8M runs clean at 96K exec/s.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:09:00.993667137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:10:00Z","close_reason":"Shipped: fuzz_statx.rs registered in Cargo.toml, builds clean, 5.8M runs at 96K exec/s over 60s, no crashes (initial harness bug - too-strict stx_mask invariant - corrected pre-ship).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-7bha","title":"Expand stdio_file_ops conformance: fgetc, fputc, fgets, fputs, feof, ferror, fileno","description":"stdio_file_ops.json only tests 9 functions with 15 cases. Many implemented stdio functions lack coverage: fgetc/fputc (char I/O), fgets/fputs (string I/O), feof/ferror (stream state), fileno, clearerr, ungetc, rewind, fgetpos/fsetpos. Add differential testing for character and string I/O operations.","status":"done","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:43:27.317706169Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T17:48:35.498014481Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-7c192","title":"PORT: 16 NIS+ nis_* fail-safe libnsl stubs (error formatters + local queries + free no-ops)","description":"Add 16 NIS+ (nis_*) fail-safe stubs to extend the libnsl parity work. NIS+ is even deader than NIS — sun deprecated it in the late 1990s — but binaries that link against it through libc must still resolve at link-edit. Provide the cheapest possible safe behavior. Error helpers (4): nis_perror writes "<label>: NIS+ error" to fd 2; nis_sperrno returns a static description string for the given nis_error code; nis_sperror returns a malloc-allocated "<label>: <msg>" string; nis_sperror_r writes that into a caller buffer. Logger (1): nis_lerror is a no-op (syslog target). Free helpers (7): nis_freeresult / nis_freenames / nis_free_object / nis_free_directory / nis_free_request / nis_freeservlist / nis_freetags are all no-ops because our query stubs never allocate any of those structures. Local queries (4): nis_local_directory / nis_local_host / nis_local_principal / nis_local_group all return a pointer to a static empty C string. Error codes use the standard NIS+ enum (NIS_NAMEUNREACHABLE = 5 etc). 16 functions total.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:23:45.435848978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:23:45.435848978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-7c192","title":"PORT: 16 NIS+ nis_* fail-safe libnsl stubs (error formatters + local queries + free no-ops)","description":"Add 16 NIS+ (nis_*) fail-safe stubs to extend the libnsl parity work. NIS+ is even deader than NIS — sun deprecated it in the late 1990s — but binaries that link against it through libc must still resolve at link-edit. Provide the cheapest possible safe behavior. Error helpers (4): nis_perror writes "<label>: NIS+ error" to fd 2; nis_sperrno returns a static description string for the given nis_error code; nis_sperror returns a malloc-allocated "<label>: <msg>" string; nis_sperror_r writes that into a caller buffer. Logger (1): nis_lerror is a no-op (syslog target). Free helpers (7): nis_freeresult / nis_freenames / nis_free_object / nis_free_directory / nis_free_request / nis_freeservlist / nis_freetags are all no-ops because our query stubs never allocate any of those structures. Local queries (4): nis_local_directory / nis_local_host / nis_local_principal / nis_local_group all return a pointer to a static empty C string. Error codes use the standard NIS+ enum (NIS_NAMEUNREACHABLE = 5 etc). 16 functions total.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:23:45.435848978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:28:02.125538165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:28:02.123408894Z","close_reason":"shipped: 16 NIS+ nis_* stubs (4 local queries + 7 free no-ops + 4 error formatters + nis_lerror) + 10 abi tests covering empty-string returns + NULL/bogus-pointer no-op safety + sperror text matches + ERANGE/EINVAL paths","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-7cba","title":"bd-3bg subtask: Minimal codec scope/exclusion ledger + compatibility intent","description":"Background:
- iconv scope must be explicit to avoid overpromising full iconvdata parity before deterministic generation pipeline exists.

Goal:
- Define minimal codec scope, exclusions, and compatibility intent for phase progression.

Deliverables:
1) Codec inclusion/exclusion ledger with rationale.
2) Compatibility goals per included codec family.
3) Support matrix mapping for supported/unsupported codecs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Scope is explicit and user-facing claims are accurate.
- No ambiguous codec support status.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for scope manifest parsing/validation.
- Structured logs for scope checks and drift detections.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyMink","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:01:54.290717538Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:23.346392084Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:23.346371766Z","close_reason":"Implemented deterministic iconv codec scope ledger drift gate, structured evidence logs, and CI integration","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","iconv","locale","spec","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1057,"issue_id":"bd-7cba","author":"IndigoMaple","text":"Progress update: implemented iconv codec scope/exclusion ledger and drift gates.

Changes:
- Added tests/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.v1.json (included/excluded codec ledger + rationale + support-matrix mapping).
- Added crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test.rs (schema, set consistency, exclusion rationale, support-matrix alignment checks).
- Updated crates/frankenlibc-core/src/iconv/mod.rs with explicit phase-1 codec constants + normalization/out-of-scope tests.
- Updated support_matrix.json iconv strict semantics to include UTF-32.
- Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md iconv hard-parts entry to reference ledger artifact.

Validation:
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core iconv_open_rejects_out_of_scope_codecs -- --nocapture (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test -- --nocapture (pass)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails on pre-existing unrelated frankenlibc-abi lints/warnings)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (fails due pre-existing formatting diffs in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/grp_abi.rs and crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/pwd_abi.rs).","created_at":"2026-02-15T19:28:09Z"},{"id":1058,"issue_id":"bd-7cba","author":"HazyMink","text":"Progress update (2026-02-26): added deterministic iconv scope-ledger drift gate and wired it into extended CI.\n\nChanges:\n- Added scripts/check_iconv_codec_scope_ledger.sh (bd-7cba gate).\n- Gate validates ledger schema/identity, included+excluded codec sets vs core iconv phase-1 constants, alias normalization coverage, and support_matrix semantics alignment for iconv/iconv_open/iconv_close.\n- Gate emits structured evidence artifacts: target/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.report.json and target/conformance/iconv_codec_scope_ledger.log.jsonl plus tests/cve_arena/results/bd-7cba/{trace.jsonl,artifact_index.json}.\n- Wired gate into scripts/ci.sh extended gates after iconv table generation gate.\n\nValidation:\n- scripts/check_iconv_codec_scope_ledger.sh -> PASS\n- rch check -> PASS (8/8 workers healthy)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test iconv_codec_scope_ledger_test -> PASS (4 passed)\n- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-core iconv_open_rejects_out_of_scope_codecs -> PASS (1 passed)\n\nNote: observed unrelated pre-existing warnings in frankenlibc-abi/src/stdbit_abi.rs during test build output; no changes made in that surface.","created_at":"2026-02-26T04:41:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-7dw2","title":"bd-rqn subtask: Decision-law linkage proof suite for all production controllers","description":"Background:
- Production controllers must be proven to actually influence runtime decisions in a measurable and auditable way.

Goal:
- Validate decision-law linkage for each production controller and detect dead/ornamental wiring.

Deliverables:
1) Linkage tests that show controller signals reach decide/observe outcomes.
2) Snapshot/report fields demonstrating active influence.
3) Dead-link detection gate for production set.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every production controller has passing linkage evidence.
- Dead/inactive production controller wiring fails gates.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit and integration tests for linkage paths.
- Structured logs with trace_id, controller_id, signal, influence_result, artifact_refs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:38.573199672Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T23:24:09.950090999Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T23:24:09.811469213Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","math","runtime","testing","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1059,"issue_id":"bd-7dw2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-7dw2.

Plan:
1) Define the authoritative production-controller set from  + .
2) Implement a harness-side linkage test that, for each production controller, proves:
   - it is reachable from the hot-path decision law ( / observe hooks), and
   - its cached state is actually read in fusion/severity aggregation (not dead code).
3) Add a deterministic gate script (extended CI) that fails if any production controller lacks linkage proof artifacts.
4) Emit structured JSONL logs: trace_id, controller_id, probe_case, observed_state_delta, influence_result, artifact_refs.

Will coordinate via Agent Mail once MCP load clears (currently health_level=red).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:04Z"},{"id":1060,"issue_id":"bd-7dw2","author":"Codex","text":"Starting bd-7dw2 (corrected comment; prior comment had zsh backtick substitution).

2) Implement a harness-side linkage proof suite that, for each production controller, proves:

   - its cached state is actually read on the hot path (fusion/severity aggregation), not dead/ornamental wiring.
3) Add a deterministic gate script (extended CI) that fails if any production controller lacks linkage proof artifacts.
4) Emit structured JSONL logs with: trace_id, controller_id, probe_case, observed_state_delta, influence_result, artifact_refs.

Coordination: will send Agent Mail updates once MCP load clears (currently health_level=red).","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:51:49Z"},{"id":1061,"issue_id":"bd-7dw2","author":"Codex","text":"Closed bd-7dw2.

Delivered:
- Harness subcommand: frankenlibc-harness runtime-math-linkage-proofs
- Gate script: scripts/check_runtime_math_linkage_proofs.sh
- CI wiring: scripts/ci.sh (extended gates)
- Integration tests: crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/runtime_math_linkage_proofs_test.rs

Structured artifacts:

Verification:

- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_linkage_proofs_test -- --nocapture

Notes:
- The proof gate enforces: every production module in tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json must show decision-law influence (direct decide read or fusion-input linkage). Dead/ornamental wiring fails deterministically with per-module diagnostics.","created_at":"2026-02-12T23:24:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-7ef9","title":"bd-h5x subtask: Callthrough census + decommission sequencing by symbol family","description":"Background:
- Callthrough elimination work needs symbol-family-level truth to avoid hidden dependence on host glibc.

Goal:
- Produce current callthrough census and decommission plan by family/symbol with risk and sequence.

Deliverables:
1) Enumerate all remaining callthrough symbols and their owning modules.
2) Rank by runtime impact and replacement complexity.
3) Define migration order with rollback strategy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Census is machine-readable and tied to support matrix.
- Decommission plan is dependency-aware and implementation-ready.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for census extraction/parsing helpers.
- Structured logs for census generation and ranking decisions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberStone","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:03:32.598635951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:07.423915839Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:07.423897114Z","close_reason":"Completed support-matrix-tied callthrough census artifact with dependency-aware decommission waves, generator/check scripts, structured log/report emission, and passing gate/verification evidence.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["callthrough","critique","docs","syscall","verification"],"comments":[{"id":1062,"issue_id":"bd-7ef9","author":"AmberStone","text":"Implemented deterministic callthrough census + dependency-aware decommission sequencing tied to support_matrix.json.\n\nArtifacts:\n- tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json\n- scripts/generate_callthrough_census.py\n- scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh\n- crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/callthrough_census_test.rs\n- target/conformance/callthrough_census.report.json (generated by gate)\n- target/conformance/callthrough_census.log.jsonl (generated by gate)\n\nValidation:\n- python3 scripts/generate_callthrough_census.py --support-matrix support_matrix.json --output tests/conformance/callthrough_census.v1.json\n- scripts/check_callthrough_census.sh\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-amber cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test callthrough_census_test --no-run\n- /tmp/cargo-target-amber/debug/deps/callthrough_census_test-13fe70897b726eac\n- CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/cargo-target-amber cargo run -p frankenlibc-harness --bin harness -- verify --fixture tests/conformance/fixtures --report /tmp/frankenlibc_strict_hardened_verify_bd_7ef9.md\n\nNotes:\n- Artifact captures support-matrix summary drift explicitly (: summary reports 54 vs 49 derived callthrough symbols).\n- Direct 
running 3 tests
test artifact_exists_and_has_required_shape ... ok
test artifact_summary_counts_match_rows ... ok
test gate_script_passes_and_emits_artifacts ... ok

test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.11s hit an intermittent linker bus error in rust-lld while building bin harness in this environment; test binary itself executed cleanly after .","created_at":"2026-02-13T08:51:00Z"}]}

-  "total_exported": 4010,
